C# expose C-core's debug error string in Status struct #22891

author: markdroth
text: Another interesting case we should consider: If the resolver never returns a result and RPCs fail due to DEADLINE\_EXCEEDED, the error message should indicate that the resolver never returned a result.

author: apolcyn
text:
Another interesting case we should consider: If the resolver never returns a result and RPCs fail due to DEADLINE\_EXCEEDED, the error message should indicate that the resolver never returned a result.

I agree, I also think this "waiting to acquire resource X" would be helpful debug info for a lot of other things that are currently buried in logs. For example, there are a number of interesting things that an RPC can "stall" on:

If these things could represented e.g. with some counters, and be accounted for on a per-RPC basis, then they could then be tied back to each RPC's context and be surfaced up to users either in an RPC's error message or perhaps alongside it - IMO that would useful to debug why some RPC's fail and potentially also why some RPC's are slow (e.g. determining that some specific high latency RPC took a long time because it was spending all of it's time waiting for name resolution to finish).

I'm not sure if perhaps the census context could be leveraged for these kinds of things....

author: markdroth
text:
This issue is only about things that cause RPCs to fail, so it would not include things like flow control. Those things are better diagnosed by (e.g.) the application noticing that a send\_message op takes too long to complete.

I don't think we need to use census for this, and I don't think we need a new API; I think it's sufficient to just include the relevant information in the status message we return along with the status code (typically UNAVAILABLE for these cases).

But regardless of what API we use to return the information, the important thing is to structure our internals such that all of the information is available at the point at which the status is being generated.

author: markdroth
text: Another example: When a wait\_for\_ready RPC hits its deadline, the status message should include the error from the last LB pick.

author: markdroth
text: In most LB policies, when we set state to TRANSIENT\_FAILURE, we probably want to return just the leaf status (i.e., the status from the last child policy or subchannel that reported a connection failure). May (or may not) want to do something different for the xds\_routing policy, since it may be useful to report which route we attempted to take in addition to the leaf error.

author: markdroth
text:
Once [#30283](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/pull/30283) is merged, I think we'll be generating useful RPC status messages in all of our LB policies.

The remaining work here is to improve the messages for DEADLINE\_EXCEEDED cases and to provide better messages for UNAVAILABLE statuses generated by the transport.

author: copybara-service
text:
copybara-serviceadded a commit that references this issue on May 31, 2024[grpc_error] remove unnecessary status attributes (#36523)...34ac4ee
[grpc_error] remove unnecessary status attributes (#36523)

The following attributes were completely unused:
- kOffset
- kIndex
- kSize
- kFilename
- kKey
- kValue

The following attributes were added but never programmatically accessed, and I've moved them into the status messages themselves, which is another step toward #22883:
- kErrorNo
- kTsiCode
- kWsaError
- kHttpStatus
- kOsError
- kSyscall
- kTargetAddress
- kRawBytes
- kTsiError

Closes #36523
